questions.q: Which avatar of Vishnu is Krishna counted as in the traditional list?
questions.explanation: Krishna is reckoned the eighth avatar of Vishnu, born to relieve the burdened Earth.

questions.q: What role did Krishna take at Arjuna's side in the Kurukshetra war?
questions.explanation: Having vowed not to bear arms, Krishna drove Arjuna's chariot through all eighteen days of battle.

questions.q: What teaching did Krishna deliver to Arjuna between the two armies?
questions.explanation: When Arjuna dropped his bow in despair, Krishna spoke the seven hundred verses of the Bhagavad Gita.

questions.q: Which city did Krishna build for the Yadavas on the western sea?
questions.explanation: Krishna raised the fortress city of Dwarka on the western ocean, built by the divine architect Vishvakarma.

questions.q: What was the name of Krishna's conch?
questions.explanation: Krishna's conch Panchajanya, taken from the sea demon Panchajana, sounded the opening of the war.

questions.q: Which sister of Krishna married Arjuna?
questions.explanation: Krishna steered his sister Subhadra toward Arjuna, who carried her off with her consent and Krishna's blessing.

questions.q: Who was Krishna's mighty elder brother?
questions.explanation: Balarama, the plough-bearer and incarnation of Shesha, was Krishna's elder brother.

questions.q: Before the war, what choice did Krishna offer Arjuna and Duryodhana?
options: His treasury to one, his fleet to the other
His blessing to one, his curse to the other
Dwarka to one side, Mathura to the other
His vast army to one side, himself alone and unarmed to the other
questions.explanation: Krishna offered his Narayani army to one claimant and only his own unarmed self to the other.

questions.q: Who chose the unarmed Krishna over his entire army?
questions.explanation: Arjuna chose Krishna alone, while Duryodhana left delighted with the great Narayani host.

questions.q: What was the name of Krishna's discus?
questions.explanation: Krishna wielded the Sudarshana Chakra, the spinning discus that returned to his hand after every throw.

questions.q: Who were Krishna's birth parents?
questions.explanation: Krishna was born to Vasudeva and Devaki in Kamsa's prison and raised in secret by Nanda and Yashoda.

questions.q: Which tyrant uncle did Krishna slay in Mathura?
questions.explanation: Krishna killed Kamsa, Devaki's brother, who had murdered her earlier children to escape a prophecy of his own death.

questions.q: Why did Krishna move the Yadavas from Mathura to Dwarka?
options: Jarasandha's repeated sieges made Mathura unsafe
A famine had emptied the Mathura countryside
A sage cursed Mathura to sink into the Yamuna
Kamsa's allies had seized the city's gates
questions.explanation: Jarasandha of Magadha, father-in-law of the slain Kamsa, besieged Mathura again and again until Krishna moved his people west.

questions.q: Whom did Krishna behead with his discus at Yudhishthira's Rajasuya?
questions.explanation: When Shishupala's insults passed the hundredth, Krishna loosed the Sudarshana and took off the Chedi king's head.

questions.q: As peace envoy, what minimal demand did Krishna make of Duryodhana for the Pandavas?
questions.explanation: Krishna asked for only five villages, and Duryodhana refused even land enough for the point of a needle.

questions.q: What did Krishna reveal when Duryodhana tried to seize him at the Kuru court?
options: The Sudarshana poised above the throne
questions.explanation: Krishna displayed the Vishvarupa, his cosmic form, blinding the court and showing the folly of binding a god.

questions.q: At whose humble home did Krishna dine in Hastinapura, declining Duryodhana's feast?
questions.explanation: Krishna chose the simple food of the devoted Vidura over Duryodhana's royal hospitality offered without love.

questions.q: In the most retold version, how did Krishna save Draupadi in the dice hall?
options: He appeared and struck down Dushasana
By his grace her sari grew without end
He sent Garuda to carry her away
He froze the assembly with a single glance
questions.explanation: Popular retellings say Krishna's grace made the sari endless as Dushasana pulled; the critical edition credits unseen dharma.

questions.q: What curse did Gandhari lay upon Krishna after the war?
options: That he would be reborn a hundred times
That Dwarka would burn that very night
That his clan would destroy itself in thirty-six years and he would die alone
That he would forget his divinity forever
questions.explanation: Grieving her hundred sons, Gandhari cursed the Yadavas to mutual slaughter in thirty-six years, and Krishna accepted it with a smile.

questions.q: How did Krishna's mortal life end?
options: He was slain in the iron-pestle brawl at Prabhasa
He drowned when the sea swallowed Dwarka
He renounced his body in yogic trance on a mountaintop
A hunter named Jara pierced his heel with an arrow, mistaking him for a deer
questions.explanation: As Krishna rested beneath a tree, the hunter Jara mistook his foot for a deer and shot the arrow that ended his earthly life.

questions.q: What was Kunti's relation to Krishna?
options: She was his father Vasudeva's sister, making him her nephew
She was his mother Devaki's cousin by marriage
She was his foster mother Yashoda's sister
She was unrelated, bound to him only by alliance
questions.explanation: Kunti, born Pritha, was Vasudeva's sister, so Krishna was the Pandavas' cousin through their mother.

questions.q: Krishna's false-sunset stratagem helped Arjuna keep his vow to kill which king?
questions.explanation: When darkness seemed to fall, Jayadratha's guards relaxed, the light returned, and Arjuna's arrow fulfilled the sunset vow.

questions.q: Why did Krishna endure a hundred insults from Shishupala before killing him?
options: He was bound by a vow of nonviolence at sacrifices
Bhishma had begged mercy for the Chedi king
He had promised Shishupala's mother to forgive a hundred offenses
The Rajasuya rites forbade bloodshed until sundown
questions.explanation: Krishna had promised his aunt, Shishupala's mother, to pardon a hundred offenses, and the hundred-and-first was answered with the discus.

questions.q: What secret did Krishna disclose to Karna before the war began?
options: The day appointed for Bhishma's fall
The mortal flaw in Duryodhana's body
The hiding place of the Pandava weapons
That Karna was Kunti's firstborn and elder brother to the Pandavas
questions.explanation: Krishna revealed Karna's birth and offered him the throne as the eldest Pandava, but Karna chose loyalty to Duryodhana.

questions.q: How did Krishna break his own vow of not bearing arms during the war?
options: He charged at Bhishma with a weapon, restrained only by Arjuna's pleas
He hurled the Sudarshana at Drona's formation
He shot a single arrow to save Yudhishthira
He swung a mace against Shalya's guard
questions.explanation: Enraged by Bhishma's onslaught, Krishna leapt down and rushed at him — with a chariot wheel in popular tradition, a whip or the discus in the texts — until Arjuna held him back.

questions.q: How did Bhishma respond when Krishna rushed at him in fury?
options: He fled the field until sunset
He welcomed death at the god's hands, lowering his own weapons in reverence
He reminded Krishna of his vow and shamed him into retreat
He called on Shikhandi to shield him
questions.explanation: Bhishma dropped his bow and welcomed the attack, declaring that death at Krishna's hands would be his highest glory.

questions.q: What counsel of Krishna saved the Pandava army from the Narayanastra?
options: To scatter in every direction until the storm passed
To raise the Pashupatastra in counter
To lay down arms, dismount, and abandon even the wish to fight
To shelter behind a wall of elephants
questions.explanation: The Narayanastra grew fiercer against resistance, so Krishna ordered total surrender of weapons and will, and the storm passed over.

questions.q: Whom did Krishna revive by a solemn act of truth after the war?
options: Ghatotkacha, after the dart had flown
The five sleeping sons of Draupadi
The stillborn Parikshit, struck in the womb by the Brahmastra
questions.explanation: Krishna revived Uttara's stillborn son Parikshit, blasted in the womb by Ashwatthama's weapon, preserving the Kuru line.

questions.q: How did Krishna engineer the death of Jarasandha?
options: He entered Girivraja disguised as a brahmin with Bhima and Arjuna and had Bhima wrestle him
He lured him into a sea battle off the coast of Dwarka
He turned Jarasandha's vassal kings against him with gifts
He challenged him to single combat with the discus
questions.explanation: Disguised as brahmins, the three entered Magadha's capital, and at Krishna's prompting Bhima tore Jarasandha apart in a wrestling match.

questions.q: In the pairing of the ancient seers reborn on the battlefield, Krishna was Narayana; who was Nara?
questions.explanation: The epic identifies Krishna and Arjuna as the ancient seers Narayana and Nara, inseparable across the ages.
